unit.name: Deep learning revival, ImageNet-era breakthroughs, and why the field exploded
unit.description: Explains how modern deep learning transformed computer vision through large datasets, neural architectures, and compute, while also expanding to many applications and raising ethical concerns.
unit.summary: The lecture recounts the neural-network branch of the timeline from perceptrons and backpropagation to LeNet and the gradual re-emergence of 'deep learning' in the 2000s [slides show perceptron, backprop, LeNet, and early deep learning milestones around the mid-lecture timeline]. Li presents ImageNet as a turning point for scalable visual recognition and shows how the AlexNet era made deep learning dominant in image classification and then across retrieval, detection, segmentation, activity recognition, captioning, relationship understanding, style transfer, and image generation [ts=1450s] [ts=2353s]. She emphasizes that the modern explosion came from three converging forces—computation, algorithms, and data—which pulled the field out of AI winter [ts=2420s] [ts=2446s]. The segment also highlights the role of hardware improvements, especially rapid GPU progress in FLOPs per dollar, and connects this to the broader acceleration of AI conferences, startups, and enterprise applications [ts=2448s] [ts=2515s]. It closes by warning that computer vision is still unsolved and that data-driven systems can carry bias and materially affect people's lives in areas like face recognition, jobs, loans, and medicine [ts=2522s] [ts=2611s].

key_points.text: Backpropagation enabled effective training of multilayer neural networks, paving the way for modern deep learning.
ImageNet-scale recognition marked a decisive shift that made deep learning central to computer vision.
Modern computer vision expanded from classification into retrieval, detection, segmentation, activity recognition, captioning, relationship understanding, style transfer, and image generation.
The modern AI boom is attributed to the convergence of computation, algorithms, and data.
Rapid GPU hardware progress strongly accelerated deep learning adoption.
Bias in data can propagate into vision systems, especially in face recognition and other high-stakes uses.

kp_catalog.name: Deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale
kp_catalog.description: Why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty. Large-scale datasets, GPU computation, and neural architectures converged to unlock modern deep learning for vision.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because deep learning resurgence from data, compute, and scale formalizes how why modern neural NLP depends on a set of training and modeling ideas that made deep networks workable after years of difficulty, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Modern vision extends beyond classification
kp_catalog.description: The course treats vision as covering segmentation, detection, video, multimodal learning, generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because modern vision extends beyond classification formalizes how the course treats vision as covering segmentation, detection, video, multimodal learning, generative models, vision-language models, 3D vision, and embodied intelligence, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Human-centered AI
kp_catalog.description: The lecture frames AI progress through the lens of human perception, human impact, and human values rather than only algorithmic performance. Vision systems can save lives and create value, but also propagate bias and cause harm in high-stakes settings.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because human-centered AI formalizes how the lecture frames AI progress through the lens of human perception, human impact, and human values rather than only algorithmic performance, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context though roles split between capstone and gateway.
